methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $60M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

private.name: Type One Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series B), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Blykalla is a Series B nuclear energy company founded by Janne Wallenius and Peter Szakalos that has raised $60M+ to date. The nuclear energy sector contains 3 public comps (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers including Type One Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo, though the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or valuation-tier clustering for reliable median benchmarking.

Given data constraints, only stage-progression methodology is reliably applicable. Using total raised ($60M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.9x step-up multiplier for Series B progression yields an implied valuation of $114M. This reflects the capital invested to date and incorporates standard progression assumptions for the stage, absent more granular peer comparables within the narrow nuclear energy frontier-tech window.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $97M to $131M, centered on $114M. Key uncertainties include the absence of sufficient same-stage private comps to validate peer-median approaches, reliance on stage-progression as a single methodological pillar, and the heterogeneity of nuclear technology approaches across the peer set (modular reactors, fast reactors, and advanced fuel cycles command different risk profiles). Any valuation in this range should be stress-tested against company-specific technical, regulatory, and capital-path assumptions.
